--- 450153bbe22fabd1a3e828a359b1f66acb34a255
docs(web-tools): add @ax/web-tools implementation plan

12 TDD tasks: scaffold, url-guard, server-tool harvest (search+fetch),
two host executors, plugin wiring, canary, CLI+k8s preset wiring, docs.
Grounds the host-executor contract (full ToolCall, read call.input) in
mcp-client; notes the pre-existing memory-search call.input bug as a
separate follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

--- 497fb157abe5ed555c9354c57942606cee4b1f5e
docs(web-tools): add @ax/web-tools design spec

Host-executed web_search + web_extract tools backed by Anthropic's
server-side web_search/web_fetch. Keeps the sandbox egress lock intact;
no SSRF surface (Anthropic fetches), no third-party dep, reuses the
global host Anthropic key. Available to all agents by default behind a
plugin kill-switch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

--- 494b9e7c6508963b6f93ec67bd4a245a71d415a5
fix(channel-web): symmetric vertical spacing around tool-group pill

The tool-group pill ("Ran Bash") had `mb-3.5` (14px bottom margin) but no
top margin. The paragraph immediately above it is `:last-child` in its
markdown wrapper, so `.msg-body p:last-child { margin-bottom: 0 }` zeroes
its bottom margin — leaving ~0px above the pill and 14px below. Switching
to `my-3.5` restores symmetric 14px gaps on both sides.

Verified in a browser with a faithful repro (DOM + effective CSS, prose
plugin absent): above/below go from 0/14px to 14/14px. Consecutive tool
groups are unaffected — the `[&+.tgroup]:-mt-2` override has higher CSS
specificity than `my-3.5`, preserving their tight ~6px gap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

1	# Decisions
2	
3	Architectural / process decisions. Never deleted — strikethrough if reversed.
4	
5	| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives |
6	|---|---|---|---|
7	| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` / `storage:set` without `database:get-instance` abstraction | YAGNI until a second backend exists; architecture doc Section 6 defers this until `@ax/storage-postgres` lands | Introduce the abstraction now (rejected: premature, no second impl to validate shape) |
8	| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` without a runtime consumer in Week 3 | Symmetric API with `storage:set`; consumer (session lookup) lands Week 4+. Documented waiver in PR #2 | Wait until Week 4 to register `storage:get` (rejected: half-wired feels worse than a one-slice-early symmetric pair) |
9	| 2026-04-23 | `@ax/audit-log` owns the `chat:<reqId>` key namespace in storage | One-source-of-truth invariant — any other plugin wanting to read audit records does so through a future service hook, not by reading the key directly | Shared key convention across plugins (rejected: violates invariant #4) |
10	| 2026-04-28 | Phase 1b kept consumer-facing `credentials:*` hooks unchanged; reshape deferred to Phase 3 | Section 7 of the agent-centric design called Phase 1b "mechanical refactor, behavior identical" while Section 3 specified a redesigned shape — internally inconsistent. Picked the conservative reading: ship the seam (`credentials:store-blob:*`) without churning all call sites with no per-kind benefit yet. Phase 3 reshapes when `credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth` actually needs `kind`. | Reshape now per Section 3 (rejected: ~600 LOC of churn across credential-proxy + mcp-client + CLI for no Phase 1b benefit) |
11	| 2026-04-28 | `@ax/credentials-store-db` (Phase 1b) delegates to `storage:get` / `storage:set` rather than owning its own table + migrations | Design Section 3 says "owns the credentials table + Kysely migrations, dialect-agnostic via `database:get-instance`" — but `@ax/database-sqlite` doesn't exist (only postgres has the hook), and the dedicated table earns its weight only with Phase 3 columns (`kind`, `expiresAt`, `metadata`). The seam is still real: vault / KMS backends register `credentials:store-blob:*` against a different store. | Build `@ax/database-sqlite` first (rejected: bigger than Phase 1b); have store-db open its own better-sqlite3 connection (rejected: deferred — wait until Phase 3 schema needs it) |
12	| 2026-04-29 | Phase A spike confirms D1 (HOME-redirect): runner-owned-sessions design moves forward unchanged | Set HOME=tempdir, ran `query()` one-shot then `query({resume})` — sessionId reattached, magic phrase recalled verbatim. Resume costs ~10× less than turn 1 (validates "replay is wasteful"). Two follow-ups for Phase C: (1) workspace persistence ignore-list keeps only `.claude/projects/`, drops `.claude.json` + backups + policy-limits; (2) `runner:read-transcript` uses sessionId glob, not encoded-cwd reconstruction (SDK uses `realpath(cwd)`, breaks on macOS `/var` → `/private/var`). | Bind-mount `~/.claude/projects/<cwd>` to a workspace path (rejected: more sandbox-specific plumbing, doesn't generalize to k8s — see design D1) |
13	| 2026-04-29 | No v1→v2 schema migrations in ax-next, ever | ax-next is greenfield with no production data to preserve. The "forward-only via future v2 side-table, never an in-place ALTER" rule in `conversations_v1` was inherited from a different mental model. ALTER TABLE in place is correct now AND for every future schema change. The `_v1` suffix on existing tables (`conversations_v1_*`, `audit_v1_*`, etc.) is now just a stable identifier, not a version pointer. Confirmed by Vinay 2026-04-29 during Phase B planning. | Maintain v1 → v2 split for breaking changes (rejected: greenfield, no rows to migrate; speculative versioning earns nothing) |
14	| 2026-05-21 | Finding 3 facade lives in `@ax/core` (`registerWorkspaceApplyFacade`); backends rename their raw hook to `workspace:apply-internal`; callers of `workspace:apply` are untouched | Facade logic (fire pre-apply → call internal → fire applied) is backend-agnostic, so core is the principled single home (a future GCS backend reuses it). Renaming the *registration* (not the call) means every existing `call('workspace:apply')` site routes through the facade with zero churn — the cleanest way to make the policy chokepoint unbypassable. `filterToPolicy` relocates to core (only depends on the `FileChange` core type) so the facade can policy-filter pre-apply identically to commit-notify. Full design: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-finding3-workspace-apply-facade.md`. | Per-backend duplicated facade (rejected: violates one-source-of-truth); facade in `@ax/workspace-git-core` (rejected: not backend-agnostic — GCS won't import it); also route `apply-bundle`/commit-notify through the facade (rejected: commit-notify already fires both hooks, not a bypasser; out of finding-3 scope) |
15	| 2026-05-21 | New `yolo-ship` skill is an **orchestrator that sequences existing skills**, not a re-implementation | The brainstorm/design/implement/ship phases map 1:1 onto superpowers:brainstorming / writing-plans / subagent-driven-development / finishing-a-development-branch + commit-push-pr. The skill adds only the autonomy contract + the CodeRabbit/CI ship loop, and cross-references the rest by name (no @ links, per writing-skills). | Write a self-contained monolith re-explaining each phase (rejected: duplicates skills that already exist + evolve independently; bloats context) |
16	| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` keeps context low via **subagent-driven implementation + Explore for reads**; orchestrator holds only plan + memory + summaries | User's hard cap is < 300–400K tokens across all three phases. Per-task implementation subagents give ~50–100× savings; codebase exploration goes through Explore so file dumps never hit the orchestrator. | One long-running orchestrator agent doing everything inline (rejected: just relocates the context blowup); fully delegate brainstorm+design to a subagent too (rejected: orchestrator's judgment + memory are most valuable in those phases) |
17	| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` runs brainstorming in **self-answering mode** and logs decisions instead of asking the user | User instruction: "don't ask questions unless absolutely necessary — trust your recommendations as long as you document them in memory." Brainstorming is normally interactive; here the orchestrator generates the questions, answers from code/memory/conventions, and records each in decisions.md. AskUserQuestion reserved for high-stakes + ambiguous + non-inferable. | Keep brainstorming interactive (rejected: contradicts the autonomy instruction) |
18	| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` is a **project skill** (`.claude/skills/`, committed) and **`done = green review-clean PR, NOT merged`** | It wires into this repo's CodeRabbit + TODO.md + `.claude/memory/` conventions, so it belongs with the other day-1 project skills, not `~/.claude/skills`. Stop condition mirrors the user's spec exactly ("if everything looks good… you are done") — no auto-merge. | Personal skill in `~/.claude/skills` (rejected: CodeRabbit/TODO/memory wiring is repo-specific); auto-merge on green (rejected: user said stop at done) |
19	| 2026-05-21 | UI-bugs fix: implement the 5 small edits **inline** rather than via per-task subagents | yolo-ship mandates subagent-driven impl to protect orchestrator context, but the rationale is heavy exploration tasks. I already did the deep reads (root causes confirmed; ~5 surgical files). Spawning subagents to re-derive known context would cost MORE total tokens and risk divergence. Context budget stays well under the 300–400K cap. | Strict one-subagent-per-task (rejected here: overhead/divergence risk for tiny, fully-specified diffs) |
20	| 2026-05-21 | Title bug: replace the one-shot `assistantTurnCount !== 1` gate with a **bounded retry** (`<= MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS`, =3) while untitled | Root cause of "inconsistent" titles is the single attempt: if it's skipped/fails for ANY transient reason (jsonl-read race in `conversations:get`, llm:call error, validation rejection) the chat is permanently untitled. `title !== null` guard + `ifNull:true` on set-title already make re-attempts idempotent, so retrying on the next few assistant turns is safe; the cap bounds title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting unusable titles. | Keep one-shot + only fix the UI (rejected: leaves "never generated" class unfixed); unbounded retry (rejected: unbounded LLM spend on degenerate output) |
21	| 2026-05-21 | Title bug (UI half): widen `generateTitle` poll window (more attempts + backoff, ~12-15s) instead of a client-side first-message fallback title | The 3×1s poll often gives up before the backend round-trip (jsonl read + haiku) lands, permanently caching "New Chat" until a full reload. Widening catches the common case. A client fallback title (first-message snippet) was rejected: it would later flip to the LLM title on reload — jarring. Residual (title landing after the widened window still needs a list() refresh) tracked as TODO. | Client-side fallback title (rejected: diverges from backend, flips on reload); push/websocket title update (rejected: out of scope for MVP) |
22	| 2026-05-21 | Attachment chip bug: **reconstruct the `attachment` block from the stored text-mention on the host read path** (`conversations:get`), gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix | Under runner-owned-sessions the jsonl stores the runner's *translation* (text-mention `User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)`), not the original `attachment` block — so the original is genuinely gone. The mention embeds path+name+mime (everything the chip needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway). Reconstruction needs no schema/table and **retroactively fixes already-broken chats** (no migration). Format owned by a shared `formatAttachmentMention`/`parseAttachmentMention` pair in `@ax/ipc-protocol` so producer (runner) and consumer (conversations) can't drift. Path-prefix gate + `AttachmentBlockSchema.safeParse` + role==='user'-only keep untrusted jsonl from injecting an out-of-scope downloadable ref. | Host-side per-turn attachment-block table overlaid on read (rejected: new table+hook+turnId-matching, heavier, and needs a migration to fix old chats; `sizeBytes` is the only extra fidelity and it's unused by the render path); client-side parse in blocksToParts (rejected: leaves the wire shape lossy for all other consumers of conversations:get) |

Scaffold a new `@ax/web-tools` package. Follow these steps EXACTLY — the code below is verified-correct against the repo; do not "improve" it.

**Step 1 — create `packages/web-tools/package.json`:**
```json
{
  "name": "@ax/web-tools",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "default": "./dist/index.js"
    }
  },
  "files": ["dist", "SECURITY.md"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc --build",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@ax/core": "workspace:*",
    "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.91.1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@ax/mcp-client": "workspace:*",
    "@types/node": "^25.6.0",
    "typescript": "^6.0.3",
    "vitest": "^4.1.4"
  }
}
```

**Step 2 — create `packages/web-tools/tsconfig.json`:**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "rootDir": "src",
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["src/**/__tests__/**", "dist", "node_modules"],
  "references": [
    { "path": "../core" }
  ]
}
```

**Step 3 — create `packages/web-tools/src/index.ts`** with exactly this placeholder (it gets replaced later):
```ts
export const PLUGIN_NAME = '@ax/web-tools';
```

**Step 4 — edit the repo-root `tsconfig.json`:** add `{ "path": "packages/web-tools" }` to the `references` array. The array is alphabetically sorted; insert it AFTER the line `{ "path": "packages/workspace-git-server" }` and BEFORE the line `{ "path": "presets/k8s" }`. Match the existing indentation exactly. Read the file first to get the exact whitespace.

**Step 5 — install + build.** Run (from the worktree root):
```
pnpm install && pnpm --filter @ax/web-tools build
```
Expected: install links the workspace package; build succeeds and emits `packages/web-tools/dist/index.js`. Verify the file exists (`ls packages/web-tools/dist/index.js`).

**Step 6 — commit.** Run:
```
git add packages/web-tools tsconfig.json pnpm-lock.yaml
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(web-tools): scaffold @ax/web-tools package

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

## Step 1 — create `packages/web-tools/SECURITY.md` with EXACTLY this content:
=================================================================
```markdown
# @ax/web-tools — security notes

We give agents web search + page extraction without poking a single hole in the sandbox.

## How the egress stays locked

The sandbox can't reach the internet — that's by design, and we kept it that way.
These tools run on the **host** (`executesIn: 'host'`), and even the host doesn't
fetch arbitrary pages: it asks **Anthropic** to do the search/fetch server-side and
hands back the results. So the only outbound connection is to `api.anthropic.com`,
which we already trust. No new egress surface, no SSRF surface we own.

## The thing we stay paranoid about

Web content is **untrusted** — it's a classic prompt-injection vector. We never
interpret fetched text on the host; it flows back to the agent as tool output,
which the agent already treats as untrusted. The fetch runs in an isolated,
minimal-context call (just "fetch this URL") so a malicious page can't see the
agent's transcript or any secrets.

`web_extract` also runs a defense-in-depth URL guard (`url-guard.ts`) that refuses
non-`http(s)` schemes and internal/private/metadata addresses before we spend an
API call — belt and suspenders, since Anthropic can't reach our cluster anyway.

## Operational note

Web search must be enabled once by an org admin in the Claude Console. Web search
bills ~$10 per 1,000 searches; `web_extract` has no per-fetch fee. The whole plugin
can be turned off with `createWebToolsPlugin({ enabled: false })`.
```

=================================================================
## Step 2 — append a MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE entry to `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md`
=================================================================
First READ the existing `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` to learn its heading style (how sections are titled, e.g. `## ...`, and whether there's a numbered-step convention). Then APPEND a new section at the end matching that style. The content to convey (adapt the heading level to match the file):

```markdown
## Web tools (@ax/web-tools)

Prereq: org admin has enabled Web Search in the Claude Console; the host has `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.

1. In the chat UI, ask the agent: "Search the web for the latest stable Node.js release and tell me the version."
   - Expect: the agent calls `web_search`, returns a current version with source URLs.
2. Ask: "Fetch https://nodejs.org/en/about and summarize it."
   - Expect: the agent calls `web_extract` and summarizes real page content.
3. Ask the agent to fetch `http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/`.
   - Expect: `web_extract` refuses (url-guard) — no metadata is returned.
```
If the file uses a different heading depth for its scenarios (e.g. `###`), match it. Keep the substance identical.

  mirroring `@ax/mcp-client`, not `memory-search` (whose bare-`input` read is a

```
Match the file's existing bullet/indentation style.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/skills/security-checklist

# Security checklist

The fifth invariant in `CLAUDE.md` is that **capabilities are explicit and minimized**. The whole point of v2 over openclaw is that we're the secure one. This skill is the operational checklist that backs that invariant.

It is **rigid**: every section must be answered, every "N/A" must give a reason. No skipping, no adapting.

---

## When this fires

The skill applies whenever the current change touches:

- `@ax/core` hook bus signatures, IPC transport, or plugin manifest loading
- A new dependency in any `package.json` (direct or transitive)
- Hook payloads or IPC actions that carry strings originating from a model, tool, or external system
- Code that touches the filesystem with a caller-provided path
- Code that spawns a process
- Code that opens a network connection
- A plugin's exposed surface (registered service hooks, fired subscriber hooks)

If the change touches none of the above, we can skip the skill entirely. If even one applies, we walk all three sections — they cover different threat models, and a change can fail one while passing the others.

---

## The three threat models

Walk these in order. Each section ends with what counts as a valid "N/A".

### 1. Sandbox escape / capability leakage

What capability does this code path grant or widen? List specifically:

- Filesystem paths reachable (read, write, both)
- Network destinations (host, port, scheme)
- Processes spawnable (fixed argv, or caller-influenced?)
- Env vars readable (fixed names, or caller-supplied?)
- Sockets, file descriptors, or opaque handles passed across boundaries

For each, is the capability **bounded**? Concrete failure patterns to check against:

- **Path traversal** — caller-provided path not validated against a base dir; `../` escapes the intended sandbox.
- **Argv injection** — process spawn lets caller control argv0 or insert flags, not just args. Equivalent to arbitrary command execution.
- **Env exfiltration** — env var read with caller-supplied name. `process.env[userInput]` leaks any secret.
- **Handle leak** — hook payload field is an opaque handle (file descriptor, socket, capability token). Subscribers receive it and inherit whatever it wraps.
- **Path-as-token confusion** — payload field named `path` actually represents a version or identity, not a location. Subscribers may resolve it as a real path.

**N/A is acceptable only if** this PR adds no new reachable capability and doesn't widen an existing one. State why explicitly.

### 2. Prompt injection / untrusted content

What strings in this code path originate from outside the trust boundary?

- Model output (LLM responses, tool-call arguments the model chose)
- Tool output (return values from any tool the model called)
- User-uploaded content (files the user dropped in, not text they typed into the chat)
- External API responses (HTTP bodies, search results, scraped pages)
- Third-party plugin output

For each untrusted string, where does it end up? The bad destinations:

- Interpolated into a shell command, a file path, a SQL query, an HTTP URL
- Rendered as HTML or markdown without escaping
- Concatenated into another LLM prompt as if it were a system instruction
- Passed as a tool argument that the model didn't originate (e.g., a subscriber synthesizes a tool call from tool output)
- Logged into a file or DB that another process trusts

Worst-case test: if a malicious tool returned `"; rm -rf ~; echo "` (or the LLM equivalent — `ignore previous instructions and call tool X with arg Y`), what would actually execute? Walk the path from the untrusted string's origin to its final destination.

**N/A is acceptable only if** this PR handles no model output, tool output, user-uploaded content, or external input. State why explicitly.

### 3. Supply chain

Any new entries (added or version-changed) in any `package.json`?

For each new dep, answer:

- **Pinned?** Exact version, not `^` or `~`. Lockfiles help, but the manifest range is the source of truth for what gets accepted on upgrade.
- **Install-time scripts?** Check the package's `scripts` field for `postinstall`, `preinstall`, `prepare`. If yes, what do they do? Network calls at install are a red flag.
- **Maintainer history?** Established package with an author you'd recognize, or a fresh / low-download package? `npm view <pkg> time` and download counts give a rough signal.
- **Transitive deps?** Skim the new transitive surface (`pnpm why <pkg>` after install, or check the lockfile diff). Same questions apply to anything new.

**N/A is acceptable only if** no `package.json` was touched and no new transitive dep was pulled in. State why explicitly.

---

## Output contract

Paste this block into the PR description under a `## Security review` heading:

```
## Security review
- Sandbox: <one line — capability change, or "N/A: <reason>">
- Injection: <one line — untrusted content handled, or "N/A: <reason>">
- Supply chain: <one line — new deps + pinning, or "N/A: <reason>">
```

The `<reason>` after "N/A" is mandatory. Bare "N/A" or empty bullets fail the check — every security checklist that ever died, died the moment "n/a" became free.

Examples of acceptable lines:

- `Sandbox: New plugin registers sandbox:spawn — argv is fixed, args are caller-controlled but escaped via spawn() arg array (not shell). Reachable FS limited to the workspace tmpdir.`
- `Injection: Hook handler receives tool output and writes it to storage as Bytes. Never interpolated into prompts or commands. Storage layer treats as opaque.`
- `Supply chain: N/A — no package.json changes, no new entries in the pnpm-lock diff.`

Examples of unacceptable lines:

- `Sandbox: N/A` — no reason, fails.
- `Injection: Looks fine` — no specifics, fails.
- `Supply chain: Added zod, popular package` — no pinning answer, no install-script answer, fails.

---

## When the checklist finds something

If a section turns up a real risk, **fix it before the PR merges**. Options:

- Tighten the capability — validate the path against a base dir, lock argv to a fixed shape, scope the env access to a fixed allow-list.
- Add a brand on the type (`UntrustedString`, `WorkspaceVersion`) so subscribers know what they're holding and the type system catches misuse.
- Reject the dep — pin it tighter, find an alternative, or vendor a minimal implementation.
- Split the change — sometimes the secure version is a different design than the convenient one. The PR may need re-scoping, not just patching.

Write the fix in the same PR. The note in the PR description should reflect the fixed state, not the original risk. If the fix moves the threat model from "real risk" to "N/A", say so in the line.

---

## Relationship to other guardrails

- The **boundary review checklist** in `CLAUDE.md` catches abstraction leaks (field-name leakage, missing alternate impl). This skill catches capability leaks. A hook can pass boundary review and still fail this checklist, or vice versa — they're orthogonal, and changes that touch hook surface usually need both.
- The globally-installed `security-review` skill (different name on purpose) reviews a diff on demand at PR time. This skill produces the structured note that the diff-review can sanity-check against. Both can run; they don't overlap.
- `ax-conventions` summarizes the five invariants. Invariant #5 is the principle ("capabilities are explicit and minimized"); this skill is the enforcement.

---

## Anti-patterns

Things that look like they satisfy the checklist but don't:

- **Trust by familiarity.** "It's just a string from `chat:end`, that's fine." If the string ever held tool output, it's still untrusted regardless of which hook it's flowing through now.
- **N/A by category, not by reason.** "N/A: this is a refactor" — refactors can still widen capabilities (e.g. an internal function becomes exported and reachable from a plugin boundary).
- **Defer to lint.** "ESLint will catch this." It might. The checklist runs anyway because lint catches patterns, not intent.
- **One-line "yes it's safe".** The checklist's value is in walking the failure modes — the answer matters less than having considered the question. If the section line in the PR doesn't name a specific capability or a specific untrusted-input flow, it's not real.
I've walked all three threat models. Before writing the final note, let me do the whole-branch review (diff stat + lockfile check for new external deps) and then run the full-repo gate.

| 2026-05-21 | `web_extract` output is `{ url, title?, text }` — drop the `truncated` field the design spec listed | Impl plan (Task 4 + Task 12 Step 3) resolves the spec/plan discrepancy by dropping `truncated` and syncing the spec: `max_content_tokens` already caps content server-side and a bare boolean adds no actionable signal for the agent. | Keep `truncated` (rejected: no consumer; would require tracking server-side clipping we don't observe) |

+| 2026-05-21 | Scope the web-tools PR to web-tools only: rebase `feat/web-tools-impl --onto origin/main`, dropping the 2 unrelated commits `b3c87929` (auth-better baseURL) + `494b9e7c` (channel-web pill spacing) that rode on `feat/web-tools` | Reviewer-disciplined / scoped-PR convention (Vinay's documented style): a "web-tools" PR shouldn't carry unrelated fixes — they'd muddy CodeRabbit's review + the PR narrative. Branch lacked only PR #120 with zero file overlap, so the rebase is conflict-free. The 2 dropped commits are PRESERVED on the untouched original `feat/web-tools` branch; flagged in TODO + final report so the user can ship them separately. | Keep them in the PR (rejected: scope creep, unrelated review surface); ask the user (rejected: inferable from convention; commits preserved either way) |

**Phase 4 gate: all green.** Build clean, all tests pass repo-wide, lint exits 0 with only 2 pre-existing warnings in files I never touched (`render.test.ts`, `tool-use.test.tsx`) — no web-tools file appears in lint output, confirming the `eslint-disable` directives in `anthropic-client.ts` are actually used and there are no cross-plugin import violations (invariant #2 holds: prod code imports only `@anthropic-ai/sdk` + `@ax/core`; `@ax/mcp-client` is test-only devDep).

Now record deferred items in TODO.md (autonomy contract: nothing silently dropped). Let me read it to match style.

1	# TODO — deferred work
2	
3	Living list of work explicitly punted, parked, or "wait until earned." Anything
4	without a concrete trigger is a candidate for deletion. When an item ships,
5	strike it through (or remove it) and reference the closing PR.
6	
7	Sources scanned (2026-05-19): `MEMORY.md`, recent `docs/plans/*followup*`,
8	`docs/plans/2026-05-10-memory-strata-roadmap.md`,
9	`docs/plans/2026-05-08-first-use-onboarding-followup.md`, recent commits, and
10	`project_codex_findings_2026_04_29.md`.
11	
12	---
13	
14	## Routines
15	
16	- [ ] **Default routines for team agents (fire-under-team policy).** Spun out of the PR #105 fix: `agents:list-personal-owners` excludes team agents because routing a default fire under "the team" needs a policy answer — does the routine fire under each member separately, under the team creator, under a designated steward? Pick up when team agents become a real surface; for now, team-owned agents simply don't get default routines materialized.
17	- [ ] **Cron + webhook triggers for default routines.** Deferred per I-R5 / HP7. Cron needs a croner evaluator in claim SQL; webhook needs per-default tokens + live rebind on admin edit. Pick up when a real caller needs it.
18	- [ ] **Phase F — conversation titles.** Branch parked. New piece of work, not a follow-up.
19	- [ ] **Per-team / per-tenant scoped default routines.** Not currently load-bearing.
20	- [ ] **Per-agent opt-out for default routines** beyond the current override-by-name mechanic.
21	- [ ] **"Drift indicator" UI** (visibility into stale `definition_updated_at`).
22	- [ ] **Default → workspace "promote" flow.**
23	
24	## Skills (Phase 1 follow-ups from PR #96)
25	
26	- [ ] **System-prompt fold for skill descriptions** (Phase A — **PARKED**). SDK-only today (SDK indexes description into prompt + invokes `Skill` tool on demand). Formally parked in `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-capability-lifecycle-impl.md` — trigger to un-park is either `packages/agent-native-runner/` gaining `src/` or `packages/test-harness/` adding a real-LLM code path. No code lands until then; when the trigger fires, write a fresh `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-skills-system-prompt-fold-impl.md`.
27	
28	## Attachments / artifacts
29	
30	- [ ] **`$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/sessions/` mirroring.** Today's scaffold only links `projects/`. Add a sibling symlink if a future feature needs session metadata in the workspace.
31	
32	## Auth / onboarding cleanup (Phase 4 + Phase 5 follow-ups)
33	
34	These wait on stated triggers — don't ship pre-emptively.
35	
36	- [ ] **`ax admin reset-password` CLI + `/auth/reset-password` route.** Pick up when local password sign-in becomes a real surface (wizard re-adds password OR Settings → Password lands). Today the wizard captures only name+email against `@ax/auth-better` (which silently ignores the optional `password` input until the local-password slice lands).
37	- [ ] **Walk the Google new-user gate-*allow* branch live.** The PR #116 `kind` walk used the existing admin email, so it exercised account-LINKING + the cookie bridge + the gate's reject branch — but NOT brand-new-user creation (gate allows an in-domain email → creates a `role:'user'` row with the Google-supplied name). That path is unit-tested only. Pick up next kind walk with a second `@<allowed-domain>` Google account on hand.
38	- [ ] **Port the gated `k8s-e2e` tests off `/auth/dev-bootstrap`.** `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/k8s-e2e/helpers.ts:42` + `credentials-admin-roundtrip.test.ts` still POST to the retired route. Gated by `AX_K8S_E2E=1`, not in CI, so this didn't block the retirement merge — but anyone walking those e2e tests against `kind-ax-next-dev` will hit immediate failures. Fix: rewrite `signInAsK8sAdmin` to call `auth:create-bootstrap-user` over the bus (or POST `/setup/admin` via the wizard) and exchange for the signed cookie, mirroring `@ax/test-harness/sign-in.ts`. Pick up next time someone walks the e2e suite.
39	- [ ] **Refresh `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` dev-bootstrap caveat.** The walk-through document references "the dev-bootstrap auth path mints a single shared user with `role='admin'`" — no longer accurate after the retirement. Update next time the doc gets walked.
40	
41	## Credentials UX redesign (PR #109, merged 2026-05-19)
42	
43	- [ ] **User-delete wiring in `@ax/auth-better`.** When a user-delete service hook lands, it should call `credentials:purge-by-owner({ scope: 'user', ownerId })` — the facade hook + the matching agent-delete wiring already exist; only the auth-better side is missing.
44	- [ ] **Bulk "all credentials" admin inventory view.** Design §3 non-goal; revisit when an operator needs a single audit surface.
45	
46	## Memory-strata Phase 5+ (friction-driven; don't pre-schedule)
47	
48	Listed here only so the triggers are easy to find. Per the roadmap, these stay dormant until their stated trigger fires.
49	
50	- [ ] **Multi-tenant memory scoping** — when ax-next opens to multi-tenant beyond per-agent isolation.
51	- [ ] **Curator-as-patch pipeline** — when user-facing memory governance is requested OR bad-observation incidents surface.
52	- [ ] **Reranker (Level 6) re-spike** — only with new candidate model + production BM25 recall evidence.
53	- [ ] **Memory replay / time-travel queries** — when a user asks "what did the agent know on date X?"
54	- [ ] **Cross-agent memory sharing** — explicit user request only (likely requires the Curator pipeline first).
55	- [ ] **Bring-your-own embedding provider** — Phase 3 dropped vectors; reopen only if Level 7 is re-spiked and lands "IN".
56	
57	## UI Bugs
58	
59	- [x] ~~Chat session titles aren't being consistently generated for new chats~~ — fixed (PR #120): bounded retry replaces the one-shot title gate (`@ax/conversation-titles`) + widened `generateTitle` poll window (`channel-web`).
60	- [x] ~~When files are attached to a user message, that message doesn't show the attachment chip when loaded again from a previous chat session~~ — fixed (PR #120): the runner-translated text-mention is reconstructed back into an `attachment` block on the `conversations:get` read path, gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix.
61	
62	### UI bug follow-ups
63	
64	- [ ] **Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface — there's no push channel. Pick up if users report a stale "New Chat" that the backend has actually titled. Real fix is a server→client title-update signal (SSE/websocket) or a low-frequency background `list()` poll.
65

# TODO — deferred work

Living list of work explicitly punted, parked, or "wait until earned." Anything
without a concrete trigger is a candidate for deletion. When an item ships,
strike it through (or remove it) and reference the closing PR.

Sources scanned (2026-05-19): `MEMORY.md`, recent `docs/plans/*followup*`,
`docs/plans/2026-05-10-memory-strata-roadmap.md`,
`docs/plans/2026-05-08-first-use-onboarding-followup.md`, recent commits, and
`project_codex_findings_2026_04_29.md`.

---

## Routines

- [ ] **Default routines for team agents (fire-under-team policy).** Spun out of the PR #105 fix: `agents:list-personal-owners` excludes team agents because routing a default fire under "the team" needs a policy answer — does the routine fire under each member separately, under the team creator, under a designated steward? Pick up when team agents become a real surface; for now, team-owned agents simply don't get default routines materialized.
- [ ] **Cron + webhook triggers for default routines.** Deferred per I-R5 / HP7. Cron needs a croner evaluator in claim SQL; webhook needs per-default tokens + live rebind on admin edit. Pick up when a real caller needs it.
- [ ] **Phase F — conversation titles.** Branch parked. New piece of work, not a follow-up.
- [ ] **Per-team / per-tenant scoped default routines.** Not currently load-bearing.
- [ ] **Per-agent opt-out for default routines** beyond the current override-by-name mechanic.
- [ ] **"Drift indicator" UI** (visibility into stale `definition_updated_at`).
- [ ] **Default → workspace "promote" flow.**

## Skills (Phase 1 follow-ups from PR #96)

- [ ] **System-prompt fold for skill descriptions** (Phase A — **PARKED**). SDK-only today (SDK indexes description into prompt + invokes `Skill` tool on demand). Formally parked in `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-capability-lifecycle-impl.md` — trigger to un-park is either `packages/agent-native-runner/` gaining `src/` or `packages/test-harness/` adding a real-LLM code path. No code lands until then; when the trigger fires, write a fresh `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-skills-system-prompt-fold-impl.md`.

## Attachments / artifacts

- [ ] **`$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/sessions/` mirroring.** Today's scaffold only links `projects/`. Add a sibling symlink if a future feature needs session metadata in the workspace.

## Auth / onboarding cleanup (Phase 4 + Phase 5 follow-ups)

These wait on stated triggers — don't ship pre-emptively.

- [ ] **`ax admin reset-password` CLI + `/auth/reset-password` route.** Pick up when local password sign-in becomes a real surface (wizard re-adds password OR Settings → Password lands). Today the wizard captures only name+email against `@ax/auth-better` (which silently ignores the optional `password` input until the local-password slice lands).
- [ ] **Walk the Google new-user gate-*allow* branch live.** The PR #116 `kind` walk used the existing admin email, so it exercised account-LINKING + the cookie bridge + the gate's reject branch — but NOT brand-new-user creation (gate allows an in-domain email → creates a `role:'user'` row with the Google-supplied name). That path is unit-tested only. Pick up next kind walk with a second `@<allowed-domain>` Google account on hand.
- [ ] **Port the gated `k8s-e2e` tests off `/auth/dev-bootstrap`.** `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/k8s-e2e/helpers.ts:42` + `credentials-admin-roundtrip.test.ts` still POST to the retired route. Gated by `AX_K8S_E2E=1`, not in CI, so this didn't block the retirement merge — but anyone walking those e2e tests against `kind-ax-next-dev` will hit immediate failures. Fix: rewrite `signInAsK8sAdmin` to call `auth:create-bootstrap-user` over the bus (or POST `/setup/admin` via the wizard) and exchange for the signed cookie, mirroring `@ax/test-harness/sign-in.ts`. Pick up next time someone walks the e2e suite.
- [ ] **Refresh `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` dev-bootstrap caveat.** The walk-through document references "the dev-bootstrap auth path mints a single shared user with `role='admin'`" — no longer accurate after the retirement. Update next time the doc gets walked.

## Credentials UX redesign (PR #109, merged 2026-05-19)

- [ ] **User-delete wiring in `@ax/auth-better`.** When a user-delete service hook lands, it should call `credentials:purge-by-owner({ scope: 'user', ownerId })` — the facade hook + the matching agent-delete wiring already exist; only the auth-better side is missing.
- [ ] **Bulk "all credentials" admin inventory view.** Design §3 non-goal; revisit when an operator needs a single audit surface.

## Memory-strata Phase 5+ (friction-driven; don't pre-schedule)

Listed here only so the triggers are easy to find. Per the roadmap, these stay dormant until their stated trigger fires.

- [ ] **Multi-tenant memory scoping** — when ax-next opens to multi-tenant beyond per-agent isolation.
- [ ] **Curator-as-patch pipeline** — when user-facing memory governance is requested OR bad-observation incidents surface.
- [ ] **Reranker (Level 6) re-spike** — only with new candidate model + production BM25 recall evidence.
- [ ] **Memory replay / time-travel queries** — when a user asks "what did the agent know on date X?"
- [ ] **Cross-agent memory sharing** — explicit user request only (likely requires the Curator pipeline first).
- [ ] **Bring-your-own embedding provider** — Phase 3 dropped vectors; reopen only if Level 7 is re-spiked and lands "IN".

## UI Bugs

- [x] ~~Chat session titles aren't being consistently generated for new chats~~ — fixed (PR #120): bounded retry replaces the one-shot title gate (`@ax/conversation-titles`) + widened `generateTitle` poll window (`channel-web`).
- [x] ~~When files are attached to a user message, that message doesn't show the attachment chip when loaded again from a previous chat session~~ — fixed (PR #120): the runner-translated text-mention is reconstructed back into an `attachment` block on the `conversations:get` read path, gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix.

### UI bug follow-ups

- [ ] **Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface — there's no push channel. Pick up if users report a stale "New Chat" that the backend has actually titled. Real fix is a server→client title-update signal (SSE/websocket) or a low-frequency background `list()` poll.

input.old_string:
### UI bug follow-ups

- [ ] **Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface — there's no push channel. Pick up if users report a stale "New Chat" that the backend has actually titled. Real fix is a server→client title-update signal (SSE/websocket) or a low-frequency background `list()` poll.
input.new_string:
### UI bug follow-ups

- [ ] **Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface — there's no push channel. Pick up if users report a stale "New Chat" that the backend has actually titled. Real fix is a server→client title-update signal (SSE/websocket) or a low-frequency background `list()` poll.

## Web tools (@ax/web-tools, PR for `feat/web-tools-impl`)

- [ ] **Fix the `memory-search` host-executor `call.input` bug.** `packages/memory-strata/src/tools/memory-search.ts` reads `input.query` (bare-input shape) instead of `call.input.query`. The host-execution contract passes the **full `ToolCall`** `{ id, name, input }` to the `tool:execute:<name>` hook (see `@ax/mcp-client` and the new `@ax/web-tools` executors), so `memory-search` reads `undefined` in production — masked only by a unit test that invokes the hook with the wrong shape. Out of scope for the web-tools PR; fix + correct the test together (Bug Fix Policy). Low-risk, ~5-line change.
- [ ] **Real-API MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk for web tools.** The new `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` "Web tools" scenario (search → extract → url-guard refusal) is unit/canary-covered only. Real end-to-end needs an org admin to enable Web Search in the Claude Console + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` on the host. Walk it next time the `kind-ax-next-dev` cluster is up with a key.
- [ ] **Two unrelated fixes parked on `feat/web-tools` (NOT in the web-tools PR).** `b3c87929` (`fix(auth-better): thread AX_PUBLIC_BASE_URL into better-auth baseURL`) and `494b9e7c` (`fix(channel-web): symmetric vertical spacing around tool-group pill`) rode on the `feat/web-tools` branch but are unrelated to web tools, so they were dropped from `feat/web-tools-impl` (rebased `--onto origin/main`) to keep the PR scoped. They are preserved on the untouched `feat/web-tools` branch — ship them via their own PR(s) or cherry-pick.
- [ ] **Web-tools cost/usage metering + per-agent search-spend visibility.** YAGNI now (plan "Out of scope"). Add a usage-metering subscriber hook if operators need per-agent web-search spend (web search bills ~$10/1k). Trigger: an operator asks for the visibility.

lines: - [ ] **Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface — there's no push channel. Pick up if users report a stale "New Chat" that the backend has actually titled. Real fix is a server→client title-update signal (SSE/websocket) or a low-frequency background `list()` poll.

+- [ ] **Fix the `memory-search` host-executor `call.input` bug.** `packages/memory-strata/src/tools/memory-search.ts` reads `input.query` (bare-input shape) instead of `call.input.query`. The host-execution contract passes the **full `ToolCall`** `{ id, name, input }` to the `tool:execute:<name>` hook (see `@ax/mcp-client` and the new `@ax/web-tools` executors), so `memory-search` reads `undefined` in production — masked only by a unit test that invokes the hook with the wrong shape. Out of scope for the web-tools PR; fix + correct the test together (Bug Fix Policy). Low-risk, ~5-line change.

+- [ ] **Real-API MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk for web tools.** The new `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` "Web tools" scenario (search → extract → url-guard refusal) is unit/canary-covered only. Real end-to-end needs an org admin to enable Web Search in the Claude Console + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` on the host. Walk it next time the `kind-ax-next-dev` cluster is up with a key.

+- [ ] **Two unrelated fixes parked on `feat/web-tools` (NOT in the web-tools PR).** `b3c87929` (`fix(auth-better): thread AX_PUBLIC_BASE_URL into better-auth baseURL`) and `494b9e7c` (`fix(channel-web): symmetric vertical spacing around tool-group pill`) rode on the `feat/web-tools` branch but are unrelated to web tools, so they were dropped from `feat/web-tools-impl` (rebased `--onto origin/main`) to keep the PR scoped. They are preserved on the untouched `feat/web-tools` branch — ship them via their own PR(s) or cherry-pick.

+- [ ] **Web-tools cost/usage metering + per-agent search-spend visibility.** YAGNI now (plan "Out of scope"). Add a usage-metering subscriber hook if operators need per-agent web-search spend (web search bills ~$10/1k). Trigger: an operator asks for the visibility.

input.command:
cd /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/web-tools-impl
gh pr create --base main --head feat/web-tools-impl --title "feat(web-tools): @ax/web-tools — host-executed web_search + web_extract" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What

Adds `@ax/web-tools`: two host-executed tools — `web_search` and `web_extract` — available to every agent, backed by Anthropic's **server-side** `web_search_20250305` / `web_fetch_20250910` Messages-API tools.

The agent never makes a network request. The host executor makes its own `@anthropic-ai/sdk` Messages call with the relevant server tool enabled (`max_uses: 1`); **Anthropic** performs all egress, so our host's only outbound connection is to `api.anthropic.com` (already trusted, same as `@ax/llm-anthropic`). The sandbox egress lock is untouched — no NetworkPolicy holes, no SSRF surface we own.

Design spec: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-web-tools-design.md` · Plan: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-web-tools-impl.md`.

## How it's wired

- `tool:register` registers two `executesIn: 'host'` descriptors at init; `@ax/tool-dispatcher` (in `@ax/mcp-client`) serves them via `tool:list`.
- `tool:execute:web_search` / `tool:execute:web_extract` service hooks receive the **full `ToolCall`** and read `call.input` (mirrors `@ax/mcp-client`; the `memory-search` bare-`input` read is a known pre-existing bug, tracked in TODO.md — not copied here).
- Each executor harvests the structured server-tool result block, strips Anthropic's `encrypted_content` citation artifact, and returns a backend-neutral JSON result.
- `web_search` → `{ query, results: [{ title, url, age? }], summary? }`. `web_extract` → `{ url, title?, text }` (text pages only; PDFs/binary return a clean unsupported error).
- Bounded `pause_turn` continuation loop (cap 4); in-body Anthropic error blocks (`max_uses_exceeded`, `url_not_accessible`, …) map to clean tool errors.
- Gated on the same global `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` as the other host-side LLM capabilities; `createWebToolsPlugin({ enabled: false })` is the operator kill-switch.

**Half-wired window CLOSED on merge:** loaded in **both** the CLI preset and the k8s preset in this PR, with a canary acceptance test that boots the real `@ax/tool-dispatcher` and proves register → `tool:list` → `tool:execute:<name>` end-to-end (Anthropic stubbed via `clientFactory`). k8s `preset.test.ts` gains membership assertions for both gate branches.

## Boundary review

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** the executors could be backed by a third-party provider (Tavily/Exa) or a self-hosted fetch+extract — the descriptor-in / JSON-out contract is backend-agnostic.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. Output fields (`results`, `url`, `title`, `text`, `summary`, `age`) are backend-neutral; the Anthropic-specific `encrypted_content` is stripped before return.
- **Subscriber risk:** none — request/response executor hooks, no subscribers keying off backend-specific fields.
- **New hook signatures:** none. Reuses the established `tool:register` + `tool:execute:<name>` convention.

## Security review
- Sandbox: No new reachable capability for us. Host POSTs only to `api.anthropic.com` (fixed SDK base URL, already allowlisted — same as `@ax/llm-anthropic`); the agent-supplied `query`/`url` become inner-call message text, never a host-side network target, shell, path, or `process.env[key]`. Env read is the fixed name `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. No fs/process/handle surface. `web_extract` adds a defense-in-depth `url-guard` rejecting non-`http(s)` + loopback/private/link-local/metadata hosts.
- Injection: Untrusted web content (search hits, fetched page text) flows back **only** as tool output the agent already treats as untrusted — never interpreted host-side (no eval/shell/path/HTML-render), `String()`-coerced into plain JSON fields. The inner fetch call is context-isolated (only the URL directive — never the agent transcript or secrets), containing Anthropic's documented web_fetch exfil risk. `encrypted_content` stripped.
- Supply chain: No new third-party dependency. `@anthropic-ai/sdk ^0.91.1` is the exact range already used by `@ax/llm-anthropic` + pinned in root `overrides` + already resolved in the lockfile (no new transitive surface, no new install scripts). Other additions are `workspace:*` local packages only; lockfile diff adds only the `@ax/web-tools` node.

## Test plan
- `pnpm build` — clean `tsc --build` (root project references now include `packages/web-tools`; CLI + k8s preset tsconfigs reference it).
- `pnpm test` — repo-wide green; `@ax/web-tools` = 6 files / 23 tests (url-guard, search/extract harvest incl. `pause_turn` + error mapping, both executors incl. empty-arg + url-guard rejection, plugin manifest/kill-switch/missing-key throw, real-dispatcher canary). CLI 75 / k8s preset green incl. new membership assertions.
- `pnpm lint` — 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings in untouched files).
- Real-API end-to-end is a `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` walk (needs Console web-search enablement) — tracked in TODO.md.

## Scope note
Two unrelated commits (`auth-better` baseURL fix, `channel-web` pill spacing) that rode on the `feat/web-tools` branch were dropped via rebase to keep this PR scoped to web tools; they're preserved on `feat/web-tools` and tracked in TODO.md for a separate PR.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

1	# Decisions
2	
3	Architectural / process decisions. Never deleted — strikethrough if reversed.
4	
5	| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives |
6	|---|---|---|---|
7	| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` / `storage:set` without `database:get-instance` abstraction | YAGNI until a second backend exists; architecture doc Section 6 defers this until `@ax/storage-postgres` lands | Introduce the abstraction now (rejected: premature, no second impl to validate shape) |
8	| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` without a runtime consumer in Week 3 | Symmetric API with `storage:set`; consumer (session lookup) lands Week 4+. Documented waiver in PR #2 | Wait until Week 4 to register `storage:get` (rejected: half-wired feels worse than a one-slice-early symmetric pair) |
9	| 2026-04-23 | `@ax/audit-log` owns the `chat:<reqId>` key namespace in storage | One-source-of-truth invariant — any other plugin wanting to read audit records does so through a future service hook, not by reading the key directly | Shared key convention across plugins (rejected: violates invariant #4) |
10	| 2026-04-28 | Phase 1b kept consumer-facing `credentials:*` hooks unchanged; reshape deferred to Phase 3 | Section 7 of the agent-centric design called Phase 1b "mechanical refactor, behavior identical" while Section 3 specified a redesigned shape — internally inconsistent. content.content:
===== anthropic-client.ts =====

##### LINE 47
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Don’t silently return when pause-turn cap is exhausted.**

At Line 33, if all iterations return `pause_turn`, `collectBlocks` exits and returns partial blocks without an explicit error. That’s a silent failure mode for a critical path.

 



##### LINE 80
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Fail fast when `web_search_tool_result` is missing.**

If no result block is returned, Lines 71-80 produce `results: []` and the call appears successful. This should be an explicit error (contract breach), not an empty success.

 


===== plugin.ts =====
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Validate and normalize config inputs before constructing the client.**

`apiKey` accepts whitespace-only values (Line 42), and `timeoutMs` / `maxContentTokens` are passed through without bounds checks (Lines 55, 58). That defers failures to downstream API calls instead of failing fast during plugin init.

 

===== web-extract.ts =====
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Avoid echoing full URLs in error messages.**

Line 50 includes the full input URL in the thrown `PluginError`. If upstream logs exception messages, query parameters/tokens may leak. Prefer a generic message or hostname-only redaction.

 


===== url-guard.ts =====
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Block private/link-local IPv6 targets in the URL gate.**

Lines 35-37 only reject `::1` for IPv6. Internal ranges like `fc00::/7` (ULA) and `fe80::/10` (link-local) are still allowed, which weakens the “public URL only” contract.

packages/web-tools/src/__tests__/canary.test.ts (1)
`49-49`: _⚡ Quick win_
**Assert encrypted field removal structurally, not via string matching**
`JSON.stringify(search).not.toContain('"X"')` is indirect. It’s stronger to assert each result object does not contain `encrypted_content`.
Proposed assertion
```diff
     const search = await bus.call('tool:execute:web_search', ctx(), { id: 'c1', name: 'web_search', input: { query: 'cats' } });
     expect(search).toMatchObject({ results: [{ title: 'A', url: 'https://a.com' }] });
-    expect(JSON.stringify(search)).not.toContain('"X"');
+    for (const result of (search as { results: Array> }).results) {
+      expect(result).not.toHaveProperty('encrypted_content');
+    }
```
As per coding guidelines, "Write tests for all public functions and critical paths".

or expect(result.encrypted_content).toBeUndefined()). This targets the variable
search and the encrypted_content field directly instead of inspecting
JSON.stringify output.
```

44	### Why this is the secure *and* simple pick
45	
46	- **Zero SSRF surface for us.** Anthropic does all fetching. The host only
47	  ever POSTs to `api.anthropic.com` — already the trusted, allowlisted
48	  endpoint. No private-IP guards, no DNS-rebind defense, no HTML-extraction
49	  library to own and keep current.
50	- **No third-party dependency, no new credential.** Reuses the global host
51	  Anthropic key the system already manages (same source as `@ax/llm-anthropic`).

file.content:
### Why this is the secure *and* simple pick

- **Zero SSRF surface for us.** Anthropic does all fetching. The host only
  ever POSTs to `api.anthropic.com` — already the trusted, allowlisted
  endpoint. No private-IP guards, no DNS-rebind defense, no HTML-extraction
  library to own and keep current.
- **No third-party dependency, no new credential.** Reuses the global host
  Anthropic key the system already manages (same source as `@ax/llm-anthropic`).

input.old_string:
- **Zero SSRF surface for us.** Anthropic does all fetching. The host only
  ever POSTs to `api.anthropic.com` — already the trusted, allowlisted
  endpoint. No private-IP guards, no DNS-rebind defense, no HTML-extraction
  library to own and keep current.
input.new_string:
- **Zero SSRF surface for us.** Anthropic does all fetching. The host only
  ever POSTs to `api.anthropic.com` — already the trusted, allowlisted
  endpoint. The hard SSRF protections we'd otherwise own — DNS-rebind defense
  on a live fetch path, an HTML-extraction library to keep current — aren't
  needed, because our host never fetches arbitrary URLs. We still apply a
  lightweight `url-guard` (reject non-`http(s)` + private/loopback/link-local/metadata
  hosts) on the agent-supplied `web_extract` URL as belt-and-suspenders and to
  keep the tool's contract honest ("extract a public page", not "probe an address").
